I couldn't find any information on a plant called Triticum longearistatum. It's possible that this is a misspelling or a plant that is not well-documented.

However, I can provide information on a plant called Triticum aestivum, also known as wheat. If you meant to ask about this plant, here's a paragraph about it:

Triticum aestivum, or wheat, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is commonly cultivated for its edible grains. The health benefits of wheat include providing essential nutrients such as fiber, iron, and B vitamins, and has been traditionally used to support digestive health and boost energy levels. The bioactive constituents of wheat include ferulic acid, a polyphenol with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as other phytochemicals such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with wheat include whole grain bread, pasta, and baked goods, as well as supplements such as wheat germ oil and wheat grass juice.
